name: Lab-Grown Diamond
origin: Created in controlled laboratory environments using advanced HPHT or CVD technology, replicating the natural diamond formation process.
description: Lab-grown diamonds are chemically, physically, and optically identical to mined diamonds. They possess the same fire, scintillation, and sparkle — graded by the same 4Cs (Cut, Color, Clarity, Carat). The only difference? They're created with intention, not extraction.
characteristics: Hardness: 10 (Mohs Scale) • Refractive Index: 2.42 • Dispersion: 0.044 • Brilliance: Exceptional
meaning: Symbolizes eternal love, strength, and purity. Lab-grown diamonds carry the added meaning of conscious luxury — beauty that doesn't compromise the earth.
care: Clean with warm soapy water and a soft brush. Avoid harsh chemicals. Store separately to prevent scratching other jewelry. Professional cleaning recommended every 6 months.

name: Moissanite
origin: First discovered in a meteorite crater by Dr. Henri Moissan in 1893. Today's moissanite is lab-created silicon carbide, offering extraordinary optical properties.
description: Moissanite possesses even more fire (dispersion) and brilliance than diamond, creating a rainbow of spectral colors that dance in the light. With a hardness of 9.25 on the Mohs scale, it's one of the hardest gemstones on earth — perfect for everyday wear.
characteristics: Hardness: 9.25 (Mohs Scale) • Refractive Index: 2.65 • Dispersion: 0.104 • Brilliance: Superior to Diamond
meaning: Represents cosmic beauty and innovation. Born from the stars, moissanite symbolizes forward-thinking elegance and the brilliance of modern love.
care: Clean with mild soap and warm water. Moissanite resists scratching and clouding exceptionally well. Ultrasonic cleaners are safe to use. Avoid extreme heat exposure.

name: Lab-Grown Sapphire
origin: Created using the Verneuil or Czochralski process, lab-grown sapphires have the same corundum crystal structure as natural sapphires, formed at over 2,000°C.
description: Sapphires are treasured for their stunning blue hues, though they come in virtually every color. Lab-grown sapphires offer the same 9.0 Mohs hardness and rich saturation as their natural counterparts, making them ideal for fine jewelry that lasts generations.
characteristics: Hardness: 9.0 (Mohs Scale) • Refractive Index: 1.77 • Crystal System: Trigonal • Luster: Vitreous
meaning: Historically associated with wisdom, royalty, and divine favor. Sapphire is believed to bring clarity of mind, spiritual insight, and protection to its wearer.
care: Sapphires are very durable. Clean with warm soapy water or ultrasonic cleaner. Avoid sudden temperature changes. Store in a soft pouch to maintain polish.

name: Lab-Grown Emerald
origin: Lab-grown emeralds are created through hydrothermal synthesis, mirroring the natural geological conditions where emeralds form deep within the earth's crust.
description: Prized for their lush green color, lab-grown emeralds offer exceptional clarity compared to their natural counterparts, which are almost always heavily included. The hydrothermal process creates stones with the same beryl composition and mesmerizing green hue.
characteristics: Hardness: 7.5-8.0 (Mohs Scale) • Refractive Index: 1.58 • Crystal System: Hexagonal • Color: Green (Chromium/Vanadium)
meaning: Symbol of rebirth, love, and fertility. Cleopatra's favorite gemstone, emeralds have long been associated with renewal, growth, and the promise of new beginnings.
care: Handle with care — emeralds are softer than sapphires and diamonds. Clean gently with warm soapy water only. Avoid ultrasonic cleaners and harsh chemicals. Store separately.

name: Lab-Grown Ruby
origin: Created using the same flame-fusion or flux-growth methods, lab-grown rubies are pure corundum colored by chromium — identical to the finest natural rubies.
description: Rubies are among the most coveted gemstones in history. Lab-grown rubies deliver the same intense red color and exceptional durability, without the premium of rarity. Their 9.0 Mohs hardness makes them perfect for engagement rings and daily-wear pieces.
characteristics: Hardness: 9.0 (Mohs Scale) • Refractive Index: 1.77 • Crystal System: Trigonal • Color: Red (Chromium)
meaning: The stone of passion, courage, and vitality. Rubies have been worn by warriors and royalty throughout history as symbols of power, love, and protection.
care: Very durable and easy to maintain. Clean with warm soapy water or ultrasonic cleaner. Rubies are heat-resistant but avoid sudden temperature shocks. Store in a soft cloth pouch.
